weather_report:
Night has settled softly over Subiaco and the sky is scrubbed clean, a clear, uncomplicated dark that makes the few sounds of the street feel further away. Air clarity is high, stars steady; it feels composed rather than brisk, the sort of night that invites a slow walk after dishes are done. With the sun long gone the UV is essentially absent, so there’s no bite to the dark, just a calm, dry cool. It looks pleasant and simple, a well-pressed evening with no dramas.

The forecast keeps these clear conditions through late evening and into the small hours. Come morning, if the pattern holds, expect a bright start and quick‑building sunshine; UV will rise soon after sunrise, so think hat and a touch of sunscreen if you’re outdoors a while. Dress now in light layers—a soft sweater or a jacket you don’t mind carrying later—and aim for comfort over bulk. Given the hour, it’s a fine time to tidy a corner of the house with the windows cracked for a moment of fresh air, step out to water the herbs, or make a gentle loop around the block. Early tomorrow, a coffee on a sunny stoop, a ride to the shops, or hanging the wash to catch the first light would suit the day’s mood.
